name: The Duskway Run
description: The final leg: self-jump inside Earth's blockade, wake up with the cordon closing, and put one crate on a gravel field in Charlevoix. Evade, fight, outrun, or improvise — the clock is the enemy that kills most of them. Beat 6, the capstone.

stages.objective: Clear the station and jump (J) — the Duskway's final leg. You will sleep through the crossing.
BLOCKADE SPACE — reach Charlevoix Field before the cordon closes. Dark and slow beats loud and fast. Or it doesn't. Your run.
Find Noor at the receiving shed — deliver the cache

soul_mutations.text: We ran the Duskway's final leg and put the crate on the field at Charlevoix. Stranded for the season, all of us, on the world my grandmother left. I checked the message board at the relay on the way in. I haven't told anyone what I found there.

I flew the crew through the cordon asleep and woke them inside the blockade. The captain flew the last leg like Tib on a good day. We are stranded on Earth and I have logged, privately, that I do not entirely mind.

epilogue.success:
The crate goes off the ramp and into six practiced hands. Two hundred and seven landings; four names carried; one more sentence in the argument against the crater.

The Loup-Garou winters on Earth — stranded, broke, and for the first time in the ship's scrubbed-registry life, exactly where she is supposed to be. Tib walks east to the overlook alone and doesn't talk about it. Nobody asks. Crews know things without being told.

[i]REACHLOCK: Duskway Run — thank you for flying the demo. The Veil is still cycling. The signal is still pointing somewhere. This is where the story starts.[/i]
epilogue.failure:
The Duskway is a series of gaps in the Compact's attention, and tonight the attention won.

[i]Somewhere below, a field crew waits ninety seconds past the window, kills the floodlights, and goes home in the dark.[/i]

failure_reasons.time_expired:
The cordon closes like weather. One moment there is a corridor; the next there are four picket boats where the corridor was, and a voice on the guard channel asking for a manifest you don't have.

The crate never reaches the field. Somewhere below, a crew waits ninety seconds past the window, kills the floodlights, and goes home in the dark.

[i]The Duskway doesn't punish failure. It just doesn't record it.[/i]
failure_reasons.ship_destroyed:
The Loup-Garou dies eleven kilometers above the river she was named in the language of, and the debris comes down as slow bright weather over the north shore.

Everyone dies. The pods were rated for hyperspace, not for this.

On a gravel field in Charlevoix, Noor watches the sky burn for a long moment, then kills the floodlights. Two hundred and seven attempts. The wall at Sorrow Station gets seven names; the second wall, the small one, gets the ship.

[i]They made the argument with a nuclear weapon. She answered it by still being here — until tonight.[/i]
